frameworks.name: Antifragility Assessment Framework
frameworks.description: Evaluate whether a company benefits from volatility, disorder, and stress
steps: 1. Identify stress exposure - what shocks could hit this business?
2. Assess optionality - does the company have asymmetric upside from chaos?
3. Check for convexity - limited downside with unlimited upside potential?
4. Evaluate skin in the game - is management's wealth tied to outcomes?
5. Look for via negativa - does the company avoid fragile practices?

scoring.antifragile: Benefits from volatility, has optionality, positive convexity
scoring.robust: Survives shocks unchanged, neutral to volatility
scoring.fragile: Harmed by volatility, negative convexity, hidden tail risks

frameworks.name: Barbell Portfolio Strategy
frameworks.description: Combine extremely safe assets with highly speculative ones, nothing in between

structure.speculative_side: 10-15% in asymmetric bets with massive upside potential
structure.avoid: Middle-risk investments with moderate returns and hidden tail risks

principles: 1. Maximum loss is capped at the speculative allocation
3. Speculative side captures black swan upside
4. Rebalance after big wins to lock in gains
5. Never put safe-side money at risk

frameworks.name: Hidden Fragility Detection
frameworks.description: Identify companies with dangerous hidden tail risks
red_flags: Management compensation tied to short-term metrics

frameworks.name: Convexity Analysis Framework
frameworks.description: Identify investments with favorable asymmetric payoffs

positive_convexity.description: More to gain than to lose, benefits from volatility
positive_convexity.characteristics: Limited downside, unlimited upside, loves uncertainty

negative_convexity.description: More to lose than to gain, harmed by volatility
negative_convexity.characteristics: Limited upside, unlimited downside, needs stability

frameworks.name: Tail Hedge Construction
frameworks.description: How to build protection against black swan events
methods: VIX call options or VIX ETFs
principles: 1. Spend small amounts consistently (1-2% of portfolio/year)
2. Accept the bleed in normal times as insurance cost
3. Size for the crash - hedges should pay 10-100x in crisis
4. Never try to time when to hedge
